snippet.title: Shantanu Narayen, Chairman and CEO of Adobe: People, Technology, and Creative Inspiration
snippet.description:
Shantanu Narayen, Chairman and CEO of Adobe, spoke with Sankalp Banerjee, MBA ’23, in View From The Top at Stanford GSB. Shantanu reflected on his personal evolution as a leader across his career, including the last 16 years as CEO. He discussed the importance of innovative thinking across multiple scales, seeking opportunities in setbacks, and finding creative inspiration. He also shared his perspective on the future of technology and digital experiences, including the impact of generative AI and data transparency.

“If you can create unreasonable expectations, people amaze you with their creativity.”

#gsbvftt #gsbpodcast

snippet.title: Shantanu Narayen CEO of Adobe | Success Story of Shantanu Narayen
snippet.description:
Shantanu Narayen CEO of Adobe


Shantanu Narayen was born on May 27, 1963, in Hyderabad, India.

He is the second son of a mother who taught American literature and a father who ran a plastics company.

He attended Hyderabad Public School.

He earned a bachelor's degree in electronics and communication engineering from the University College of Engineering, Osmania University in Hyderabad.

He moved to the US to complete his education, and in 1986 received a master's degree in computer science from Bowling Green State University in Ohio.

He met his wife Reni while at Bowling Green State University in the mid-1980s; she has a doctorate in clinical psychology.

In 1986 Narayen joined a Silicon Valley start-up called Measurex Automation Systems, which made computer control systems for automotive and electronics customers.

He then moved to Apple, where he was in senior management positions from 1989 to 1995.

In 1993 he received an MBA from the Haas School of Business, University of California, Berkeley.

After Apple, he served as director of desktop and collaboration products for Silicon Graphics.

In 1996 co-founded Pictra Inc., a company that pioneered the concept of digital photo sharing over the Internet.

Narayen joined Adobe in 1998 as senior vice president of worldwide product development, a position he held through 2001.

From 2001 to 2005 he was executive vice president of worldwide products.

In 2005 he was appointed president and chief operating officer.

In November 2007, Adobe announced that Bruce Chizen would step down as CEO effective December 1, 2007, to be replaced by Narayen.

As CEO, Narayen Adobe has entered the digital experiences category, an expansion that began with the company’s acquisition of Omniture in 2009.

In May 2011, Narayen received an honorary doctorate from his alma mater, Bowling Green State University.

In 2011, Barack Obama appointed him as a member of his Management Advisory Board.

In 2018 Adobe exceeded $100 billion in market cap and joined the Fortune 400 for the first time.

In 2018 it also ranked No. 13 on Forbes’ Most Innovative Companies list.

Narayen is the lead independent director on the board of directors for Pfizer and vice chairman of the US-India Strategic Partnership Forum.

In 2018, Narayen was ranked #12 on Fortune's "Businessperson of the Year" list and was deemed "Global Indian of the Year" in 2018 by The Economic Times of India.

In 2019, he was a recipient of India's Padma Shri award.

snippet.title: B20 Summit Live: AI Is Transforming Economies | AI Opportunities, Regulations
snippet.description:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) is one of the biggest technological breakthroughs of the 21st century and touches upon almost every sector of the Indian economy. The rise in digitalization and globalization has opened new avenues for AI to reshape economies in a transformational and innovative way. Technology is altering the world and is impacting society, economies and governance as never before. Hence, countries and businesses are fast waking up to utilize the potential of AI which is finding an application in diverse sectors such as retail, finance, manufacturing, healthcare, IT and e-commerce, agriculture, waste management, etc. However, AI's proliferation also warrants thoughtful regulations to address ethical concerns, data privacy, and algorithmic biases. This session will discuss and deliberate on how to reap the opportunities and address the challenges that AI has brought for Business, Talent, Societies and Environment.
On the panel: 
Brad Smith, President and Vice Chairman, Microsoft, US
N Chandrasekaran, Chair, B20 India and Executive Chairman, Tata Sons, India
Michael Miebach, Chief Executive Officer, Mastercard, USA
Arvind Krishna, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, IBM, USA
In conversation with: Shantanu Narayen, Chair and Chief Executive Officer, Adobe, USA

#b20summit #live #nchandrasekharan

snippet.title: Top Indian CEO's who did Masters in the USA
snippet.description:
In this video, we delve into the inspiring journeys of five remarkable Indian CEOs who pursued their Masters in the United States. Join us as we explore the incredible accomplishments of Sundar Pichai, Satya Nadella, Shantanu Narayen, Arvind Krishna, and Sanjay Mehrotra.

These visionary leaders have not only left an indelible mark on the global business landscape but have also become iconic figures for aspiring entrepreneurs and professionals around the world. Through their sheer determination, intellect, and relentless pursuit of excellence, they have transformed the tech industry and continue to shape the future of innovation.

Discover how Sundar Pichai, the CEO of Alphabet Inc. and its subsidiary Google, navigated his way from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) to the prestigious Stanford University.

Witness how Satya Nadella, the CEO of Microsoft, revolutionized the company and spearheaded its transformation into a cloud computing powerhouse after his studies at the University of Wisconsin and the University of Chicago.

Delve into the story of Shantanu Narayen, the CEO of Adobe Inc., who harnessed his education at the University of California, Berkeley to lead the company's creative software revolution. 

Uncover the remarkable journey of Arvind Krishna, the CEO of IBM, who leveraged his education at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign to guide one of the world's most influential technology companies.

Finally, explore the ascent of Sanjay Mehrotra, the CEO of Micron Technology, and witness how his education at Stanford University played a pivotal role in his transformative leadership in the semiconductor industry.
